audioQuality: Azure native 48kHz 96kbps MP3 with preserved paragraph boundaries and native punctuation flow

chapters.label: Full lesson narration
chapters.script:
A household receives one salary payment on the first of the month. Rent is due, groceries are needed, a loan instalment is scheduled, emergency savings must be restored, and only then can discretionary spending happen. The salary amount matters, but the order decides who is protected when the amount is short.

A payment waterfall is the contractual order in which available transaction cash is applied. It determines which fees, reserves, interest, principal, and residual claims are paid first, which can be deferred, and where shortfalls or losses appear.

Take one crore rupees of available collections. Before allocating it, the documents define what available means. The number may include scheduled interest, principal, prepayments, recoveries, investment income, and permitted support while excluding trapped, disputed, or ineligible amounts.

Senior expenses often come first because the transaction needs servicing, trusteeship, administration, account banking, and other functions to keep operating. Paying these expenses protects the machinery, but broad or uncapped fees can consume cash investors expected to receive.

Reserve replenishment may appear before or between investor claims. A reserve drawn last month can be refilled so it remains available for future shortfalls. Moving replenishment higher protects future liquidity while reducing cash available today.

Interest priority determines which note classes receive current coupon. Senior interest usually precedes junior interest. Deferred interest may accrue, compound, trigger an event, or become unrecoverable depending on the terms. The word deferred cannot be assumed to mean harmless.

Principal priority determines how note balances decline. Sequential payment retires senior principal before junior principal. Pro rata payment shares principal under stated proportions or tests. A target-balance or turbo feature can redirect excess cash toward faster amortisation.

Residual cash goes to the lowest-ranking or residual holder after prior claims and conditions are satisfied. That holder may receive substantial upside in healthy periods and absorb the first economic pain when excess spread disappears, reserves trap, or losses allocate.

Now reduce available cash from one crore to seventy lakh rupees. Expenses still take their priority. Senior interest may remain current. Junior interest may defer. No residual is released. The shortage does not hit every claim proportionately; it follows the constitutional order.

Payment priority and loss allocation are related but not identical. A junior class can be last in payment and first in loss. Principal losses may reduce overcollateralisation, residual interests, junior balances, or specified ledger accounts before reaching senior notes.

Interest and principal should remain separate unless the documents permit crossing them. Some waterfalls allow principal to cure interest shortfalls under conditions. That protects current interest while using cash that would otherwise repay principal, changing future enhancement and note life.

Triggers turn the waterfall into a state machine. While performance tests pass, residual cash may be released and principal shared. After delinquency, loss, coverage, or servicer tests fail, cash can be trapped, reserves replenished, and principal redirected to senior notes.

The exact measurement date matters. A trigger calculated before distributions can change the current payment. One calculated after distributions may affect the next period. Cure rules determine whether the transaction returns to its former state or remains locked in protective amortisation.

Suppose delinquencies cross a threshold by one basis point. Pro rata principal switches to sequential payment. Junior investors stop receiving principal even though collections are still arriving. The trigger converts a small measured change into a large contractual change.

Hedges can add or consume cash. An interest-rate swap may cover a mismatch between fixed-rate assets and floating-rate notes. Swap payments can rank high in the waterfall, and termination amounts after counterparty default may have different priority. Ignoring the hedge can misstate both liquidity and loss.

Recoveries may enter principal collections, interest collections, or another category under the documents. Timing and classification affect which claims benefit. A generic diagram labelled recoveries cannot tell the analyst where the money actually goes.

An event-of-default waterfall may differ from the ordinary waterfall. After acceleration or enforcement, collateral proceeds can be applied under a new priority. Investors need both states and the conditions that move the transaction between them.

Reserve Bank of India directions and securities disclosures can establish that priority, enhancement, and transaction terms require clear treatment. The enforceable answer for one deal remains inside its executed documents. A model should translate those words without inventing steps absent from them.

Build the payment file as a reconciliation. Start with cash in controlled accounts. Add permitted receipts. Subtract each item only once in contractual order. Record unpaid amounts, reserve movement, note-balance changes, trigger state, and ending cash. Every rupee should have one source and one destination.

An apartment association with insufficient maintenance collections faces a familiar priority decision: pay for elevator repairs before landscaping. The analogy ends where contracts begin. A securitisation waterfall is legally binding, numerically defined, and connected to investor rights and remedies.

The waterfall does not create cash or judge asset quality. It decides who experiences the truth first when available cash is less than everyone was promised.
